fact: Always wrap await calls in try/catch. Unhandled promise rejections crash Node.js.
Use optional chaining (?.) and nullish coalescing (??) to safely access nested properties.
In Node.js ESM, use import/export. In CommonJS, use require/module.exports. Never mix them in the same file.
CORS errors: add cors() middleware BEFORE route definitions. Set origin, methods, credentials explicitly.
JWT secrets must be long random strings (32+ chars). Always verify token on every protected route. Store in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage.
Load .env with require('dotenv').config() as the very FIRST line in entry point. Never commit .env to git.
bcrypt.hash() is async. Always await it. Use saltRounds = 12 minimum for production passwords.
Avoid 'any'. Use 'unknown' for truly unknown types — it forces type checking before use.
Use interfaces for object shapes (mergeable), type aliases for unions/intersections/primitives.
Async functions always return Promise<T>. Annotate return type as Promise<void>, Promise<string>, etc.
Use asyncio.run(main()) as entry point. Never call loop.run_until_complete() in newer Python (3.10+).
Relative imports (from .module import X) only work inside packages. Add __init__.py to make a directory a package.
Pydantic v2 uses model_validate() not parse_obj(). Field validators use @field_validator decorator.
Always close SQLAlchemy sessions. Use context managers (with Session() as session:) or try/finally.
FastAPI dependencies with yield are generators. Put cleanup code after yield. Use Depends() in route params.
python-jose: use jwt.encode() with algorithm='HS256'. Decode with jwt.decode() passing the secret and algorithms list.
Use Optional<T> for nullable return values. Never return null from public methods. Use Objects.requireNonNull() for validation.
Prefer constructor injection over @Autowired field injection. It makes dependencies explicit and testable.
LazyInitializationException: add @Transactional to service methods or use JOIN FETCH in JPQL queries.
@ControllerAdvice + @ExceptionHandler creates global exception handlers. Return ResponseEntity with proper HTTP status.
Use @Data, @Builder, @AllArgsConstructor, @NoArgsConstructor from Lombok to eliminate boilerplate. Add Lombok dependency to pom.xml.
JWT filter must extend OncePerRequestFilter. Add it to SecurityFilterChain BEFORE UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.
Configure CORS in SecurityConfig.java using .cors(cors -> cors.configurationSource(...)). Don't use @CrossOrigin for global config.
Circular dependency: use @Lazy on one of the injections, or refactor to introduce a third intermediary bean.
Use @ConfigurationProperties(prefix='app') with a @Configuration class for grouped settings. Type-safe and IDE-friendly.
Use @Valid on @RequestBody params. Add validation annotations (@NotNull, @Size, @Email) to DTO fields. Handle MethodArgumentNotValidException.
Wrap errors with fmt.Errorf('context: %w', err). Check with errors.Is() and errors.As() for type-safe error inspection.
Always pass context.Context to goroutines. Use context.WithCancel() to signal shutdown. Goroutines without exit conditions leak.
A nil pointer satisfying an interface is NOT nil as an interface. Always return explicit nil for interface return types.
Borrow checker errors: use clone() if you need multiple owners, or restructure to pass references (&T) instead of values.
Never use .unwrap() in production code. Use ?, match, if let, or .unwrap_or_else() for safe error handling.
Copy package.json and install dependencies BEFORE copying source code. This caches the node_modules layer and speeds up rebuilds.
Never put secrets in Dockerfile or build args — they appear in image history. Use runtime env vars or Docker secrets.
NEVER concatenate user input into SQL queries. Always use parameterized queries / prepared statements.
.env files must be in .gitignore. Rotate any secrets that were ever committed to git history.
After every code generation: check that all imports resolve, all functions are called with correct argument counts, and no logic is left as pseudocode.
